prerequisites: An active Chargify (now Maxio Advanced Billing) account.
Owner or admin permissions in the account, required to create API keys.
Your Chargify domain (the full host such as your-site.chargify.com), used for the `domain` config field.
An API Key generated for the site you want to sync, used for the `api_key` config field.

authMethods.name: HTTP Basic Authentication (API key)
authMethods.summary: Chargify (Maxio Advanced Billing) uses HTTP Basic Authentication. The connector sends the value of the `api_key` config field as the Basic Auth username and a literal x as the password, matching the documented API_Key:X format where X or x is the password placeholder. Requests target your site at https://{domain}/, so the `domain` field must contain the full host (for example your-site.chargify.com).

setupSteps.title: Find your Chargify domain
setupSteps.body: Log in to your Chargify / Maxio Advanced Billing account and open the site you want to sync. Note your full domain (for example your-site.chargify.com) and enter it in the `domain` config field. Requests are made against https://{domain}/.

setupSteps.title: Create an API Key
setupSteps.body: In the Advanced Billing app, go to Config > Integrations > API Keys and click New API Key. Optionally add a short description of how the key will be used. Copy the generated key into the `api_key` config field.

setupSteps.title: Complete the connection fields
setupSteps.body: The `username` config field is required by the connector spec; enter any non-empty value. Authentication does not use this field or the optional `password` field: the connector authenticates by sending the `api_key` value as the Basic Auth username and a fixed x as the password.

sources.title: Core Resources for Building an Integration - Maxio (Basic Authentication, API_Key:X)
